distinctive_features: Hollow structure enclosing a plume of descending brine
Resembles an inverted chimney of ice
Thin, fragile walls that thicken over time
visual_attributes.texture: Smooth and glassy exterior with a hollow interior
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Can extend from the underside of sea ice to the seafloor, depending on conditions
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Vertical tube extending downward from sea ice

domain_specific_visual.formation_environment: Beneath developing sea ice in polar oceans
domain_specific_visual.growth_pattern: Grows downward as ice accumulates around descending brine

functional_attributes.primary_function: Formation of ice around descending brine
secondary_functions: Creates transient brine pools on the seafloor
Encases and traps bottom-dwelling sea creatures

domain_specific_functional.ecological_impact: Can freeze and trap marine life on the seafloor
domain_specific_functional.temperature_dependency: Growth influenced by air and water temperature

relationships.description: Brinicles form beneath developing sea ice as brine is expelled.
The descending brine interacts with surrounding seawater to form ice.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Studied for its unique formation and impact on marine ecosystems
